On R2 (gateway for 172.16.0.0/24): outputs of 'show ip route 172.16.0.12', 'show ip arp | include 172.16.0.12', and 'show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/0/0' (to verify interface state and ARP).

plan_history.intent_description: Diagnose and restore IP connectivity to 172.16.0.12 (25 ICMP pings sent, 0 replies).
plan_history.problem: No ICMP replies from 172.16.0.12 (25/25 pings failed) — host unreachable from test source.
plan_history.fix_summary: Bring up gateway interface to 172.16.0.0/24 and refresh/replace ARP entry for 172.16.0.12 on router2. If ARP resolution still fails, add a temporary static ARP mapping.

plan_history.intent_description: Restore IP connectivity to 172.16.0.12: R2 lacks the intended static ARP on GigabitEthernet0/0/0 so it cannot resolve the host MAC; after remediation the host should respond to ICMP from R2.
plan_history.problem: Router2 (R2) does not have a static ARP entry for 172.16.0.12 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0; R2 cannot resolve the host MAC and ICMP pings fail (0/25).
plan_history.fix_summary: Add a static ARP entry on router2 mapping 172.16.0.12 to 00:11:22:33:44:55 on interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0.

remaining_issues: Static ARP mapping for 172.16.0.12 -> 00:11:22:33:44:55 does not appear to be actually configured on R2 (only an interface description was changed).
Unclear whether the host is powered/booted or whether there is Layer‑2 connectivity (switch port, VLAN, MAC learning) between R2 and the host.
